name: Scientific Method
description: The systematic process scientists use to ask questions and find answers about nature.

content.overview: A rock sinks. A wooden log floats. Working out why is the scientific method in action: notice something, guess a reason, test the guess, refine it. It's a formal process, but you already use a version of it constantly. {{viz:diagram-01}}
keyPoints: Predict: Make a hypothesis about the answer
Experiment: Test your hypothesis with observation or controlled test
Analyze: Look at the data and results
Conclude: What did you learn? Was your prediction right?
Being wrong isn't failure—it's how you learn something real
content.whyItMatters: The scientific method is how every real discovery about the universe was made — not by debating opinions, but by checking ideas against reality and accepting when they're wrong. Gravity, electricity, atoms: all discovered by running this same loop.
